% Change things from this point on

# oslo.serialization

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/oslo.serialization.svg
:alt: Latest Version
:target: https://pypi.org/project/oslo.serialization/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/oslo.serialization.svg
:alt: Downloads
:target: https://pypi.org/project/oslo.serialization/
```

The oslo.serialization library provides support for representing objects
in transmittable and storable formats, such as Base64, JSON and MessagePack.

- Free software: Apache license
- Documentation: <https://docs.openstack.org/oslo.serialization/latest/>
- Source: <https://opendev.org/openstack/oslo.serialization>
- Bugs: <https://bugs.launchpad.net/oslo.serialization>
- Release notes: <https://docs.openstack.org/releasenotes/oslo.serialization/>

modules.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils
documentation:
MessagePack related utilities.

This module provides a few things:

1. A handy registry for getting an object down to something that can be
   msgpack serialized. See {class}`.HandlerRegistry`.
2. Wrappers around {func}`.loads` and {func}`.dumps`. The {func}`.dumps`
   wrapper will automatically use
   the {py:attr}`~oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.default_registry` for
   you if needed.

:::{versionadded} 1.3
:::
Copyright (C) 2015 Yahoo! Inc. All Rights Reserved.
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"); you may
not use this file except in compliance with the License. You may obtain
a copy of the License at
http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT
WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the
License for the specific language governing permissions and limitations
under the License.

functions.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.load
documentation:
Deserialize `fp` into a Python object.

:::{versionchanged} 1.5 Added *registry* parameter.
:::

functions.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.dump
documentation:
Serialize `obj` as a messagepack formatted stream to `fp`.

:::{versionchanged} 1.5 Added *registry* parameter.
:::

functions.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.dumps
documentation:
Serialize `obj` to a messagepack formatted `str`.

:::{versionchanged} 1.5 Added *registry* parameter.
:::

functions.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.loads
documentation:
Deserialize `s` messagepack `str` into a Python object.

:::{versionchanged} 1.5 Added *registry* parameter.
:::

variables.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.default_registry
documentation:
Default, read-only/frozen registry that will be used when none is provided.

This registry has msgpack extensions for the following:

- `DateTime` objects.
- `Date` objects.
- `UUID` objects.
- `itertools.count` objects/iterators.
- `set` and `frozenset` container(s).
- `netaddr.IPAddress` objects (only if `netaddr` is importable).
- `xmlrpclib.DateTime` datetime objects.

:::{versionadded} 1.5
:::

classes.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.Interval
documentation:
Small and/or simple immutable integer/float interval class.

Interval checking is **inclusive** of the min/max boundaries.

classes.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.HandlerRegistry

methods.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.HandlerRegistry.__iter__
documentation: Iterates over **all** registered handlers.

methods.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.HandlerRegistry.register
documentation: Register a extension handler to handle its associated type.

methods.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.HandlerRegistry.__len__
documentation: Return how many extension handlers are registered.

methods.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.HandlerRegistry.__contains__
documentation: Return if any handler exists for the given identity (number).

methods.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.HandlerRegistry.copy
documentation: Deep copy the given registry (and its handlers).

methods.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.HandlerRegistry.get
documentation: Get the handler for the given numeric identity (or none).

methods.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.HandlerRegistry.match
documentation: Match the registries handlers to the given object (or none).

class_variables.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.HandlerRegistry.reserved_extension_range
documentation:
These ranges are **always** reserved for use by `oslo.serialization` and
its own add-ons extensions (these extensions are meant to be generally
applicable to all of python).

class_variables.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.HandlerRegistry.non_reserved_extension_range
documentation:
These ranges are **always** reserved for use by applications building
their own type specific handlers (the meaning of extensions in this range
will typically vary depending on application).

class_variables.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.HandlerRegistry.min_value
documentation:
Applications can assign 0 to 127 to store application (or library)
specific type handlers; see above ranges for what is reserved by this
library and what is not.

class_variables.name: oslo_serialization.msgpackutils.HandlerRegistry.max_value
documentation:
Applications can assign 0 to 127 to store application (or library)
specific type handlers; see above ranges for what is reserved by this
library and what is not.

Registry of *type* specific msgpack handlers extensions.

See: <https://github.com/msgpack/msgpack/blob/master/spec.md#formats-ext>

Do note that due to the current limitations in the msgpack python
library we can not *currently* dump/load a tuple without converting
it to a list.

This may be fixed in: <https://github.com/msgpack/msgpack-python/pull/100>

:::{versionadded} 1.5
:::

functions.name: oslo_serialization.base64.encode_as_bytes
documentation:
Encode a string using Base64.

If *s* is a text string, first encode it to *encoding* (UTF-8 by default).

```
param s

bytes or text string to be encoded

param encoding

encoding used to encode s if it's a text string

returns

Base64 encoded byte string (bytes)
```

Use encode_as_text() to get the Base64 encoded string as text.

functions.name: oslo_serialization.base64.encode_as_text
documentation:
Encode a string using Base64.

If *s* is a text string, first encode it to *encoding* (UTF-8 by default).

```
param s

bytes or text string to be encoded

param encoding

encoding used to encode s if it's a text string

returns

Base64 encoded text string (Unicode)
```

Use encode_as_bytes() to get the Base64 encoded string as bytes.

functions.name: oslo_serialization.base64.decode_as_bytes
documentation:
Decode a Base64 encoded string.

```
param encoded

bytes or text Base64 encoded string to be decoded

returns

decoded bytes string (bytes)
```

Use decode_as_text() to get the decoded string as text.

A TypeError is raised if the input is invalid (or incorrectly padded).

functions.name: oslo_serialization.base64.decode_as_text
documentation:
Decode a Base64 encoded string.

Decode the Base64 string and then decode the result from *encoding*
(UTF-8 by default).

```
param encoded

bytes or text Base64 encoded string to be decoded

returns

decoded text string (bytes)
```

Use decode_as_bytes() to get the decoded string as bytes.

modules.name: oslo_serialization.jsonutils
documentation:
JSON related utilities.

This module provides a few things:

1. A handy function for getting an object down to something that can be
   JSON serialized. See {func}`.to_primitive`.
2. Wrappers around {func}`.loads` and {func}`.dumps`. The {func}`.dumps`
   wrapper will automatically use {func}`.to_primitive` for you if needed.
3. This sets up `anyjson` to use the {func}`.loads` and {func}`.dumps`
   wrappers if `anyjson` is available.

functions.name: oslo_serialization.jsonutils.to_primitive
documentation:
Convert a complex object into primitives.

Handy for JSON serialization. We can optionally handle instances,
but since this is a recursive function, we could have cyclical
data structures.

To handle cyclical data structures we could track the actual objects
visited in a set, but not all objects are hashable. Instead we just
track the depth of the object inspections and don't go too deep.

Therefore, `convert_instances=True` is lossy ... be aware.

If the object cannot be converted to primitive, it is returned unchanged
if fallback is not set, return fallback(value) otherwise.

:::{versionchanged} 2.22 Added *fallback* parameter.
:::

:::{versionchanged} 1.3 Support UUID encoding.
:::

:::{versionchanged} 1.6 Dictionary keys are now also encoded.
:::

functions.name: oslo_serialization.jsonutils.dumps
documentation:
Serialize `obj` to a JSON formatted `str`.

```
param obj

object to be serialized

param default

function that returns a serializable version of an object,
 is used by default.

param kwargs

extra named parameters, please see documentation     of json.dumps

returns

json formatted string
```

Use dump_as_bytes() to ensure that the result type is `bytes` on Python 2
and Python 3.

functions.name: oslo_serialization.jsonutils.dump_as_bytes
documentation:
Serialize `obj` to a JSON formatted `bytes`.

```
param obj

object to be serialized

param default

function that returns a serializable version of an object,
 is used by default.

param encoding

encoding used to encode the serialized JSON output

param kwargs

extra named parameters, please see documentation     of json.dumps

returns

json formatted string
```

:::{versionadded} 1.10
:::

functions.name: oslo_serialization.jsonutils.dump
documentation:
Serialize `obj` as a JSON formatted stream to `fp`

```
param obj

object to be serialized

param fp

a .write()-supporting file-like object

param default

function that returns a serializable version of an object,
 is used by default.

param args

extra arguments, please see documentation     of json.dump

param kwargs

extra named parameters, please see documentation     of json.dump
```

:::{versionchanged} 1.3 The *default* parameter now uses :func:`to_primitive` by default.
:::

functions.name: oslo_serialization.jsonutils.loads
documentation:
Deserialize `s` (a `str` or `unicode` instance containing a JSON

```
param s

string to deserialize

param encoding

encoding used to interpret the string

param kwargs

extra named parameters, please see documentation     of json.loads

returns

python object
```

functions.name: oslo_serialization.jsonutils.load
documentation:
Deserialize `fp` to a Python object.

```
param fp

a .read() -supporting file-like object

param encoding

encoding used to interpret the string

param kwargs

extra named parameters, please see documentation     of json.loads

returns

python object
```
